name: Nature & Wildlife
description: Plants, ecosystems, and creatures of the wild

questions.q: Which eagle species is the national bird of the United States?

source.notes: Adopted 1782. Not actually bald — 'bald' comes from old English 'balde,' meaning 'white.'

questions.q: What is the largest cat species in the world?

source.notes: Siberian/Amur tigers are the largest subspecies. Up to 11 feet long, 660 lbs.

questions.q: What is the name for a porcupine's sharp defensive spines?

source.notes: Modified hairs coated with keratin. Contrary to myth, they cannot be 'thrown.'

questions.q: Which animal, notorious for building dams, is a famous aquatic engineer?

source.notes: North American beavers build lodges from sticks and mud. Teeth grow continuously.

questions.q: The word 'vulpine' relates to what animal?

source.notes: From Latin 'vulpes' (fox). 'Lupine' relates to wolves, 'ursine' to bears.

questions.q: A squirrel's nest is called a what?

source.notes: Made of twigs and leaves. Usually in tree forks. Distinct from ground burrows (earths).

questions.q: On which continent are wild meerkats native?

source.notes: Southern Africa, in the Kalahari Desert. Part of the mongoose family.

questions.q: What animal shares its name with one of the seven deadly sins?

source.notes: Native to Central/South American rainforests. The slowest mammal — move ~0.15 mph.

questions.q: How long is a typical dog's pregnancy?

source.notes: Approximately 63 days (9 weeks). Similar to cats.

questions.q: Scaly anteater is another name for which mammal?

source.notes: Only mammal with true scales. Most trafficked mammal in the world.

questions.q: What insect family contains species like the Colorado potato beetle and the deathwatch beetle?

source.notes: Coleoptera order. Most diverse order of animals on Earth — 400,000+ species.

questions.q: How many toes does a three-toed sloth have on each front foot?

source.notes: Three-toed sloths have 3 toes on all four feet. Two-toed sloths have 2 on front, 3 on back.

questions.q: Where would you find the pygmy marmoset (world's smallest monkey) in the wild?

source.notes: Also called 'finger monkey.' Weighs ~100 grams. Rainforest canopy dweller.

questions.q: Chinchillas belong to which family of animals?

source.notes: Native to the Andes Mountains. Dense, soft fur — up to 60 hairs per follicle.

questions.q: What color are the largest members of the bear family, the polar bears?

source.notes: Fur is translucent/hollow, not truly white. Skin is black to absorb heat.

questions.q: How many species of great apes are there?

source.notes: Orangutans, gorillas, chimpanzees, and bonobos. Humans are also technically great apes.

questions.q: Which animal is the only natural predator of the giant anteater?

source.notes: Jaguars and mountain lions. Anteaters lack teeth but have powerful claws.

questions.q: Aviculture is the practice of keeping and raising which type of animal?

source.notes: From Latin 'avis' (bird). Covers everything from parrot breeding to falconry.

questions.q: Angora wool comes from which animal?

source.notes: Angora rabbits and Angora goats are different animals — both produce Angora wool/mohair.

questions.q: A young shark is called what?

source.notes: Some sharks (like great whites) give birth to live pups. Others lay egg cases ('mermaid purses').
